name: Uptrace
what_it_does: Open-source APM and distributed tracing backend that ingests OpenTelemetry data via OTLP, stores it in ClickHouse for fast analytics, and exposes a REST API for querying traces, metrics, and logs.
use_cases: Self-hosting a full OpenTelemetry-compatible observability backend without Grafana/Prometheus operational complexity
Querying trace and span data via REST API to build custom dashboards or feed observability data into agent workflows
Analyzing performance bottlenecks across microservices using distributed trace visualization and service maps
Receiving metrics, logs, and traces in a single backend from any OTel-instrumented application
not_for: Teams that need a fully managed, zero-ops observability solution — self-hosted Uptrace requires ClickHouse and infrastructure management
Organizations with very large scale (multi-TB/day) who need Uptrace Cloud's enterprise tier
Teams whose toolchain is not OpenTelemetry-compatible and who need custom data formats
best_when: Best when a dev team wants self-hosted observability with native OpenTelemetry support, fast ClickHouse-backed analytics, and an API for programmatic trace/metric access — without the operational weight of a full ELK or Grafana stack.
avoid_when: Avoid when your team lacks capacity to operate ClickHouse, or when you need a plug-and-play SaaS with guaranteed uptime SLAs and enterprise support.

auth.notes: Data ingestion uses a DSN (Data Source Name) containing a project-specific token passed as a header to the OTLP endpoint. The REST query API uses a separate API token. For self-hosted deployments, authentication configuration is operator-controlled. Uptrace Cloud uses API tokens per project.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted (Apache 2.0) is fully free with no feature restrictions. Storage costs are infrastructure costs only.
pricing.paid_tiers: Uptrace Cloud starts at approximately $30/month, scaling by data volume and retention. Enterprise plans available for large-scale deployments with support SLAs.
pricing.notes: Open-source self-hosted is the primary deployment model and is completely free. ClickHouse storage is highly compressed, making self-hosted costs predictable and often cheaper than SaaS alternatives at scale.

performance.rate_limits: No platform-imposed rate limits on self-hosted deployments. Ingest throughput limited by ClickHouse capacity. Uptrace Cloud rate limits are not publicly specified.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OTLP ingestion errors are returned as gRPC status codes or HTTP error responses with limited detail. REST query API errors are more descriptive but the API surface is not fully documented for programmatic use.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: OTLP trace ingestion is naturally idempotent by trace/span ID. REST API write operations (if any) are not explicitly documented for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: OTLP endpoint URL format differs between self-hosted and Cloud deployments — agents must be configured per deployment
REST query API is less mature than the OTLP ingest path and has limited public documentation
ClickHouse must be provisioned and maintained separately for self-hosted — failure in ClickHouse brings down the entire observability stack
No webhook/alerting push mechanism for agents to subscribe to trace anomalies — agents must poll
Self-hosted version requires Docker Compose or manual deployment; no Helm chart in the main repo (community-maintained only)

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted security posture is fully operator-controlled — TLS and auth must be configured properly or data is exposed. Open-source codebase allows full audit. Cloud version enforces TLS and token-based auth.
